tools.name: subagent_spawn
tools.description:
Spawn an independent subagent to work on a task in parallel. The subagent runs autonomously and its results are reported back when complete.

Three modes:
- **Worker** (researcher, coder, planner, investigator): Completes one focused task with scoped tools.
- **Supervisor** (role: supervisor): May spawn and coordinate one level of focused workers, read their results, reconcile conflicts, and return a final synthesis.
- **Fork** (fork: true): Inherits full parent context (messages, system prompt, memory). Shares KV cache for near-free context inheritance. Always runs as general role and cannot supervise workers. Results are internal by default (send_result_to_user: false).

Use a supervisor when the parent should delegate a complete multi-agent research program instead of coordinating every worker itself.

label.description: Short human-readable label for this subagent (e.g. "Research competitor pricing")

objective.description: The task objective — what the subagent should accomplish

context.description: Optional additional context to pass to the subagent. Ignored when fork is true — forks inherit the parent's full context.

fork.description: When true, the subagent inherits the parent's full context (messages, system prompt, memory) instead of receiving only the objective + context fields. Fork mode always runs as 'general' role (the role parameter is ignored) and defaults send_result_to_user to false. Use for tasks that benefit from the full conversational context.

send_result_to_user.description: Whether to present the subagent's result to the user when it completes. Defaults to true for regular sub-agents, false for forks. Set explicitly to override.

role.description: Agent specialization that controls tool access. 'supervisor': coordinates one level of workers and synthesizes their results. 'researcher': read-only web, files, and memory. 'coder': file and bash access. 'planner': read-only analysis. 'investigator': root-cause analysis with read-only shell investigation. 'general': full access (default) but does not receive nested-delegation instructions. Ignored when fork: true (forks always use general).

tools.name: subagent_status
tools.description: Get the status of a specific subagent or list all subagents for the current conversation. Only use this when the user explicitly asks about subagent status — do NOT poll automatically, as you will be notified when subagents complete.

subagent_id.description: Optional subagent ID to query. If omitted, returns all subagents for this conversation.

label.description: The label of the subagent (alternative to subagent_id). Case-insensitive.

tools.name: subagent_abort
tools.description: Abort a running subagent by ID or label.

subagent_id.description: The ID of the subagent to abort.

label.description: The label of the subagent (alternative to subagent_id). Case-insensitive.

tools.name: subagent_message
tools.description: Send a follow-up message to a running subagent.

subagent_id.description: The ID of the subagent to send a message to.

label.description: The label of the subagent (alternative to subagent_id). Case-insensitive.

content.description: The message content to send to the subagent.

tools.name: subagent_read
tools.description: Read the full conversation output from a subagent. Use this after a subagent completes to retrieve its full work product.

subagent_id.description: The ID of the subagent whose output to read.

label.description: The label of the subagent (alternative to subagent_id). Case-insensitive.

last_n.description: Number of recent assistant messages to return. Omit to return all messages (current behavior).
